chapter_title.ar: باب مَا يُقْطَعُ مِنْ أَلَيَاتِ الضَّأْنِ وَ مَا يُقْطَعُ مِنَ الصَّيْدِ بِنِصْفَيْنِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 8 – What is cut off from the tails of the lambs and what is cut off from the hunted prey, in two halves

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرفوع - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/50)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرفوع - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/50)
text: الظَّبْيِ وَ حِمَارِ الْوَحْشِ يُعْتَرَضَانِ بِالسَّيْفِ فَيُقَدَّانِ فَقَالَ لَا بَأْسَ بِأَكْلِهِمَا مَا لَمْ يَتَحَرَّكْ أَحَدُ النِّصْفَيْنِ فَإِنْ تَحَرَّكَ أَحَدُهُمَا لَمْ يُؤْكَلِ الْآخَرُ لِأَنَّهُ مَيْتَةٌ.

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Muhammad Bin Ahmad, from Muhammad Bin Isa, from Al nazar Bin Suweyd, from one of our companions, raising it,

‘With regards to the antelope and the wild donkey hunted by the sword, so they are both cut, so he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘There is no problem with eating both of them for as long as one of the two halves is not moving. So if one of the two is moving, the other one would not be eaten as it is dead’.
en.sarwar: 6. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from al-Nadr ibn Suwayd from certain persons of our people in a marfu‘ manner the following is narrated: “About the case of a deer or wild donkey hunted with sword; if it is cut in pieces, he (the Imam) said, ‘It is not unlawful to use both pieces for food if one of them is not still moving, but if one piece moves, the other piece then is not edible because it is dead.’”
